brand.name: FAMSA Furniture
brand.vertical: Local & service — brick-and-mortar furniture, appliances, and in-house financing retail

summary: FAMSA Furniture is a 10-store Texas retailer selling furniture, appliances, and mattresses with in-house installment credit and FAMSA personal cash loans. It serves budget-conscious, largely Hispanic/Latino Texas families who need to furnish a home now but lack the upfront cash or traditional credit to do so. Its real product is access: approved financing lets families move in and pay monthly instead of all at once.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Bridge the gap between approval/purchase and a settled home — confirm the order, set delivery expectations, explain the monthly payment process, and begin the warm-up for the next room.
campaigns.trigger: Purchase completed or credit approved (order created / first financed purchase).
campaigns.segment: New financed customers; branch by language preference (English / Spanish) and by what they bought (bedroom, living room, appliances).
campaigns.rationale: FAMSA has no post-purchase sequence today, so a new family goes silent between 'approved in the store' and 'furniture arrives' — exactly the anxious window where payment confusion and buyer's remorse live. A warm onboarding email sets the tone for a 6–24 month payment relationship and is the fastest, highest-engagement win.

emails.purpose: Instant warm welcome: confirm the purchase/approval, set delivery expectations, and demystify the first monthly payment so the new customer feels secure.
emails.subject: You're approved, {{first_name}} — here's when your home arrives
emails.preview: Your delivery, your first payment, and a real person to call — all in one place.
keyPoints: Congratulate and confirm: their purchase is approved and on its way — the empty room is about to become a real living space
Set delivery expectations clearly (what to expect, typical 1–7 day window, how scheduling works)
Explain the monthly payment process plainly — when the first payment is due and how to pay — without implying any rate/terms not in approved copy
Offer a real human: reply to this email or call your store; 'Hablamos Español' so it's easy to get help in your language
Soft tease that once you're settled, FAMSA can help with the next room — no pressure

campaigns.name: Credit Pre-Qualification Conversion
campaigns.objective: Turn a free credit pre-qualification or started-but-unfinished application into a completed in-store purchase by killing credit anxiety and removing friction.
campaigns.trigger: Credit pre-qualification submitted / approved online, or credit application started but not completed, with no purchase yet.
campaigns.segment: Pre-qualified or applied leads who have not yet bought; branch by approved vs. stalled application.
campaigns.rationale: The free credit pre-qualification is FAMSA's true low-friction entry point, and 'Will I get approved?' is the #1 purchase blocker — so a lead who already cleared that gate but never came in is the single most recoverable revenue in the funnel. Today they get no follow-up.

emails.purpose: Reassure the applicant their approval is real and waiting, show the monthly payment is manageable, and drive a low-friction next step (visit a store / finish online).
emails.subject: Good news, {{first_name}} — you're pre-approved. Now pick your pieces
emails.preview: The hard part's done. Bring your approval to any Texas FAMSA and walk out furnished.
keyPoints: Lead with the reassurance that answers their biggest fear: the approval is already cleared — no second guessing
Reframe cost as a manageable monthly payment they can plan around, not a lump sum (no specific APR/terms claims without approved copy)
Make the next step tiny: bring your approval to any of the 10 Texas stores, or finish online in minutes
Add gentle urgency: pre-qualification offers don't stay open forever — come in while it's ready
Reassure with help in English or Spanish and a no-pressure salesperson on the floor

campaigns.name: Financing-Made-Simple Nurture
campaigns.objective: Convert promotional email/SMS subscribers into credit applicants by educating them on how FAMSA financing actually works and removing the mystery around approval.
campaigns.trigger: Newsletter / Text Club opt-in, or download of a 'How FAMSA financing works' guide.
campaigns.segment: Subscribers who have opted in but never applied or purchased; branch by language preference and stated room interest.
campaigns.rationale: FAMSA captures subscribers but almost certainly only blasts promotions, so cold leads never learn that financing puts a fully furnished home within reach. An educational nurture that demystifies access turns 'someday' browsers into applicants — the top-of-funnel engine the brand is missing.

emails.purpose: Deliver the financing-made-simple guide and frame the real problem: you shouldn't have to wait years or save a lump sum to furnish your home.
emails.subject: How Texas families furnish a whole home without paying all at once
emails.preview: How FAMSA financing works, what monthly payments look like, and how to get started.
keyPoints: Frame the real problem: an empty home shouldn't have to wait until you've saved thousands — access is the point
Explain in plain language how in-house installment financing works and how monthly payments make a full home affordable
Show it's for families like theirs — credit rebuilders and first-time buyers welcome (never imply guaranteed approval)
Soft, low-commitment CTA: read the guide now, with a free pre-qualification as the natural next step when ready
Bilingual-friendly framing and a warm 'knowledgeable neighbor' tone, not a hard sell

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate lapsed customers (12+ months since purchase or recently paid off) and dormant subscribers with a 'your next room is waiting' message and a fresh application path.
campaigns.trigger: 12+ months of no purchase / loan paid off with no re-purchase, or 60–90 days of email inactivity.
campaigns.segment: Lapsed past customers (reference prior purchase) separated from never-purchased dormant subscribers.
campaigns.rationale: A paid-off FAMSA customer with a proven payment history is the easiest sale in the building, yet there's no campaign prompting their next room — so they drift to Rooms To Go, Ashley, or Wayfair once their credit improves. Win-back recovers that established-trust revenue cheaply.

emails.purpose: Remind a lapsed customer of the value they got, signal their good standing makes the next purchase easy, and invite them back for the next room.
emails.subject: Your living room's set, {{first_name}} — ready for the bedroom?
emails.preview: It's been a while. Your history with FAMSA makes your next room easier than ever.
emails.cta: See what you can furnish next
keyPoints: Reference what they bought before to make it personal ('your living room' / 'your last set')
Reassure that as a returning customer with payment history, the next approval is smoother — without promising guaranteed approval
Paint the next-room outcome: the bedroom, dining set, or appliance they've been putting off
Lower friction with a fresh pre-qualification CTA and a reminder they can do it online or at any Texas store
Warm, family-forward, bilingual welcome-back tone — 'we'd love to help your family again'

campaigns.name: Loan Payoff, Anniversary & Expansion
campaigns.objective: Protect and grow the recurring installment relationship by celebrating loan payoff and converting that milestone into the next purchase or loan.
campaigns.trigger: Installment balance fully paid off; account/purchase anniversary; or a usage milestone signaling expansion readiness.
campaigns.segment: Active and recently-paid-off financed customers; branch by health — strong payment history = expansion offer, struggling = save/help.
campaigns.rationale: FAMSA's installment model is its recurring revenue, and loan payoff is a completely untapped re-purchase trigger — the exact moment a trusted customer has freed-up monthly budget and proven they can pay. Celebrating it and inviting the next room turns one-time buyers into repeat accounts.

emails.purpose: Celebrate the payoff milestone, reinforce the trust built, and frame their established history as the door to furnishing the next room or accessing a FAMSA loan.
emails.subject: You did it, {{first_name}} — your FAMSA balance is paid off 🎉
emails.preview: Congratulations on finishing strong. Here's a thank-you and what your history unlocks.
keyPoints: Genuinely celebrate the payoff — it's a real accomplishment for a budget-conscious family and a relationship milestone, not just a transaction
Reinforce the trust: their on-time history with FAMSA is exactly what makes the next purchase easier (no guaranteed-approval claims)
Surface the expansion paths: add a room, upgrade an appliance, or a FAMSA personal cash loan for other needs
Include a thank-you incentive or VIP gesture to reward loyalty and prompt a return visit
Warm, proud, bilingual tone that treats them as a valued returning family — and an easy CTA to start the next room online or in-store
